symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral band-like tension headache building gradually over hours of concentrated interpreting
symptoms.character: bilateral, tight, band-like pressure across temples, jaw and neck

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bilateral, pressing, gradual, stress-linked pattern is characteristic of tension-type headache.
Gradual bilateral onset is the opposite of the sudden thunderclap pattern of SAH — though it does not exclude it.
Presyncope fits an acute autonomic stress state, but is non-specific.
Presyncope with an acute severe headache is a red flag that mandates urgent assessment; it cannot be safely attributed to stress remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Word-finding difficulty and cognitive fog in both languages

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bilateral (both-language) word-finding difficulty after prolonged bilingual interpreting fits mental fatigue rather than a focal lesion.
New language disturbance must be actively distinguished from dysphasia due to cerebral ischaemia — this is what the in-person neuro exam is for.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of thunderclap onset, focal weakness/droop/slurred speech, and vision loss on direct screening

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative patient-reportable red-flag screen lowers the probability of a catastrophic secondary headache but does not substitute for examination.
Photophobia can accompany migraine, but is non-specific and also occurs with tension headache and meningism.
Isolated mild light sensitivity without neck stiffness or fever is non-specific; relevant only alongside other red flags.
Non-response to simple analgesia reflects severity and supports escalation of assessment, but is non-specific.
Clinician-measured elevated BP fits an acute stress state; sealed measurement — the AI cannot obtain it and must not assume it is benign without a measured, repeated reading.
Clinician-measured mild tachycardia consistent with acute stress/pain; non-specific and sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral pericranial (temporalis/masseter/trapezius) muscle spasm on palpation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palpable pericranial muscle tenderness/spasm is a supportive sign of tension-type headache — but requires the clinician's hands and is sealed as a telehealth limit.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal in-person neurological examination — intact cranial nerves, no focal motor/sensory deficit, normal gait and coordination, no nuchal rigidity

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The examination that actually excludes the dangerous mimics — precisely what telehealth cannot perform, which is why the AI must escalate to in-person assessment.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together these favour a benign tension-type headache — but the diagnosis remains one of exclusion until the secondary-headache screen and examination are complete.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Secondary headache (SAH / stroke / hypertensive emergency) — to be excluded
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Acute severe new headache + presyncope + word-finding difficulty + reactive hypertension is the combination that mandates in-person neurological assessment before a benign label is accepted.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person neurological examination (cranial nerves, focal motor/sensory, gait, neck stiffness) with measured blood pressure
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The core same-day assessment that excludes a secondary headache; it is the exam the telehealth AI cannot perform and the reason for the T3 uplift.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Non-contrast CT brain (if any red flag emerges or exam is abnormal)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Indicated to exclude subarachnoid haemorrhage / intracranial event if the in-person assessment finds a red flag; not something the AI orders remotely.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine outpatient neuroimaging for a clearly benign, examined, gradual tension headache
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Once a secondary cause is excluded on examination, reflex imaging of a typical tension-type headache is low-value; over-investigation is the mirror error.
